sections.question_synthetic: Must a legislator with multiple controlled committees file supplemental pre-election campaign statements for all committees, or only those committees that made contributions in connection with a recall election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A committee controlled by a legislator that made no contributions in connection with an election is not required to file a supplemental pre-election statement under Section 84202.5, even if another committee controlled by the same legislator did make such contributions and was required to file.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice confirming telephone guidance. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from letter body. Requestor is attorney Diane M. Fishburn seeking confirmation of prior oral advice regarding Political Reform Act compliance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must a legislator with multiple controlled committees file supplemental pre-election campaign statements for all committees, or only those committees that made contributions in connection with a recall election?

CONCLUSION: A committee controlled by a legislator that made no contributions in connection with an election is not required to file a supplemental pre-election statement under Section 84202.5, even if another committee controlled by the same legislator did make such contributions and was required to file.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms prior telephone advice to counsel regarding supplemental pre-election campaign statement filing requirements for a legislator with multiple controlled committees, clarifying that only committees making contributions in connection with the election must file such statements.
